Add a GPG key to a specific GitLab user (admin only). Requires user ID and GPG public key.

See also: gitlab_list_gpg_keys_for_user, gitlab_delete_gpg_key_for_user

Returns: JSON with the created GPG key details.

**Parameters:**

- `key` (string) (required): The armored GPG public key content,required
- `user_id` (integer) (required): The ID of the user,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_group_badge

Add a new badge to a group. Badge URLs support placeholders like %{project_path}, %{default_branch}, %{commit_sha}.

See also: gitlab_list_group_badges, gitlab_preview_group_badge

Returns: JSON with the badge details.

**Parameters:**

- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `image_url` (string) (required): Badge image URL (supports placeholders),required
- `link_url` (string) (required): Badge link URL (supports placeholders),required
- `name` (string): Badge name

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_group_job_token_allowlist

Add a group to the CI/CD job token allowlist.

Returns: JSON with the allowlist entry.

See also: gitlab_list_job_token_group_allowlist, gitlab_remove_group_job_token_allowlist

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `target_group_id` (integer) (required): ID of the group to add to the allowlist,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_issue_discussion_note

Add a reply note to an existing issue discussion thread.

Returns: JSON with the created reply note. See also: gitlab_get_issue_discussion.

**Parameters:**

- `body` (string) (required): Note body (Markdown supported),required
- `discussion_id` (string) (required): Discussion ID to reply to,required
- `issue_iid` (integer) (required): Issue internal ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_license

Add a new GitLab license (admin). Requires the Base64-encoded license string.

Returns: JSON with the added license details.

See also: gitlab_get_license.

**Parameters:**

- `license` (string) (required): The license string (Base64-encoded),required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_merge_request_to_merge_train

Add a merge request to a merge train.

Returns: JSON array of merge train entries after the addition.

See also: gitlab_get_merge_request_on_merge_train, gitlab_list_project_merge_trains

**Parameters:**

- `auto_merge` (boolean): Enable auto-merge when pipeline succeeds
- `merge_request_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string): Head SHA of the merge request to verify
- `squash` (boolean): Squash commits when merging

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_project_badge

Add a new badge to a project. Badge URLs support placeholders like %{project_path}, %{default_branch}, %{commit_sha}.

See also: gitlab_list_project_badges, gitlab_preview_project_badge

Returns: JSON with the badge details.

**Parameters:**

- `image_url` (string) (required): Badge image URL (supports placeholders),required
- `link_url` (string) (required): Badge link URL (supports placeholders),required
- `name` (string): Badge name
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_project_job_token_allowlist

Add a project to the CI/CD job token inbound allowlist.

Returns: JSON with the allowlist entry.

See also: gitlab_list_job_token_inbound_allowlist, gitlab_remove_project_job_token_allowlist

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `target_project_id` (integer) (required): ID of the project to add to the allowlist,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_project_mirror

Create a new remote push mirror for a GitLab project. The mirror URL should include credentials if needed.

Returns: JSON with created mirror details. See also: gitlab_edit_project_mirror.

**Parameters:**

- `auth_method` (string): Authentication method (password or ssh_public_key)
- `enabled` (): Whether the mirror is enabled
- `host_keys` (): SSH host keys for the remote mirror
- `keep_divergent_refs` (): Keep divergent refs on the remote
- `mirror_branch_regex` (string): Regex pattern for branches to mirror
- `only_protected_branches` (): Mirror only protected branches
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `url` (string) (required): Remote mirror URL (e.g. https://user:token@example.com/repo.git),required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_snippet_discussion_note

Add a reply note to an existing snippet discussion thread.

Returns: JSON with the created reply note.

See also: gitlab_get_snippet_discussion, gitlab_update_snippet_discussion_note

**Parameters:**

- `body` (string) (required): Note body (Markdown supported),required
- `discussion_id` (string) (required): Discussion ID to reply to,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `snippet_id` (integer) (required): Snippet ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_ssh_key

Add an SSH key to the currently authenticated GitLab user. Requires a title and the public key content.

See also: gitlab_list_ssh_keys, gitlab_delete_ssh_key

Returns: JSON with the created SSH key details.

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): Expiration date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `key` (string) (required): The SSH public key content,required
- `title` (string) (required): A descriptive title for the SSH key,required
- `usage_type` (string): Usage type: auth or signing (default: auth)

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_ssh_key_for_user

Add an SSH key to a specific GitLab user (admin only). Requires user ID, title, and public key content.

See also: gitlab_list_ssh_keys_for_user, gitlab_delete_ssh_key_for_user

Returns: JSON with the created SSH key details.

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): Expiration date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `key` (string) (required): The SSH public key content,required
- `title` (string) (required): A descriptive title for the SSH key,required
- `usage_type` (string): Usage type: auth or signing (default: auth)
- `user_id` (integer) (required): The ID of the user,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_add_system_hook

Add a new system hook (admin). Requires URL. Optionally configure event subscriptions and SSL verification.

Returns: JSON with the created system hook details.

See also: gitlab_list_system_hooks, gitlab_delete_system_hook

**Parameters:**

- `branch_filter_strategy` (string): Branch filter strategy: wildcard, regex, or all_branches
- `description` (string): Description for the hook
- `enable_ssl_verification` (): Enable SSL verification for the hook URL
- `merge_requests_events` (): Trigger on merge request events
- `name` (string): Descriptive name for the hook
- `push_events` (): Trigger on push events
- `push_events_branch_filter` (string): Branch filter for push events (wildcard, regex, or branch name)
- `repository_update_events` (): Trigger on repository update events
- `tag_push_events` (): Trigger on tag push events
- `token` (string): Secret token for payload validation
- `url` (string) (required): Hook URL to receive events,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

### branch (7 tools)

#### gitlab_branch_create

Create a new Git branch in a GitLab project from a ref (branch name, tag name, or commit SHA). Returns: branch name, merged/protected/default status, web URL, and latest commit ID. See also: gitlab_mr_create, gitlab_branch_list.

**Parameters:**

- `branch_name` (string) (required): New branch name (param 'branch_name' not 'branch' or 'name'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string) (required): Branch name, tag, or commit SHA to create from,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_branch_delete

Delete a branch from a GitLab repository. Cannot delete the default branch or protected branches.

**Parameters:**

- `branch_name` (string) (required): Branch name to delete,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_branch_delete_merged

Delete all branches that have been merged into the default branch. The default branch and protected branches are never deleted.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_branch_list, gitlab_mr_list.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_branch_get

Returns: name, merged, protected, default status, web_url, and latest commit_id. See also: gitlab_branch_create, gitlab_commit_list.

**Parameters:**

- `branch_name` (string) (required): Branch name to retrieve (param 'branch_name' not 'branch'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

#### gitlab_branch_list

List Git branches in a GitLab project. Supports optional name search filter. Returns paginated results including each branch's protection status and latest commit info.

Returns: paginated list of branches with name, protected status, and latest commit info. See also: gitlab_branch_get, gitlab_branc...

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `search` (string): Filter branches by name (substring match)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_branch_protect

Protect a GitLab repository branch by setting push and merge access levels (0=no access, 30=developer, 40=maintainer, 60=admin). Protected branches cannot be force-pushed or deleted. Returns: branch name, push/merge access levels, allow_force_push, and code_owner_approval_required. See also: gitlab_...

**Parameters:**

- `allow_force_push` (): Allow force push to this branch
- `branch_name` (string) (required): Branch name or wildcard (e.g. 'main' or 'release/*'),required
- `code_owner_approval_required` (): Require CODEOWNERS approval for changes to matching files
- `merge_access_level` (integer): Access level for merge (0=No access 30=Developer 40=Maintainer)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `push_access_level` (integer): Access level for push (0=No access 30=Developer 40=Maintainer)

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_branch_unprotect

Remove all protection rules from a GitLab branch, allowing unrestricted push, merge, and force-push access.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_branch_protect, gitlab_protected_branches_list.

**Parameters:**

- `branch_name` (string) (required): Name of the protected branch to remove,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### ci (7 tools)

#### gitlab_ci_lint

Validate arbitrary CI/CD YAML content within a project's namespace context. Useful for testing CI configuration changes before committing. Returns validation status, errors, warnings, and merged YAML.

Returns: JSON with validation status, errors, warnings, and merged YAML.

See also: gitlab_ci_lint...

**Parameters:**

- `content` (string) (required): CI/CD YAML content to validate,required
- `dry_run` () (required): Run pipeline creation simulation
- `include_jobs` () (required): Include expanded job list in the response
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path (namespace context),required
- `ref` (string) (required): Branch or tag to use for CI includes resolution

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

- `content_ref` (string) (required): Branch or tag to use for the CI configuration content
- `dry_run` () (required): Run pipeline creation simulation
- `dry_run_ref` (string) (required): Branch or tag to use as context for the dry run
- `include_jobs` () (required): Include expanded job list in the response
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string) (required): Branch or tag to use for CI includes resolution

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_ci_variable_create

Create a new CI/CD variable in a GitLab project. Requires key and value. Optionally set type (env_var/file), protection, masking, and environment scope. Returns: key, value, variable_type, protected, masked, environment_scope. See also: gitlab_ci_variable_list.

**Parameters:**

- `description` (string) (required): Variable description
- `environment_scope` (string) (required): Environment scope (default: *)
- `key` (string) (required): Variable key name,required
- `masked` () (required): Mask variable value in job logs
- `masked_and_hidden` () (required): Mask and hide variable value
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `protected` () (required): Only expose in protected branches/tags
- `raw` () (required): Treat variable value as raw string
- `value` (string) (required): Variable value,required
- `variable_type` (string) (required): Variable type: env_var or file

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)

- `environment_scope` (string) (required): Filter by environment scope
- `key` (string) (required): Variable key name,required

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `protected` () (required): Only expose in protected branches/tags

- `value` (string) (required): Updated variable value
- `variable_type` (string) (required): Variable type: env_var or file

### commit (13 tools)

#### gitlab_commit_cherry_pick

Cherry-pick a commit to a target branch. Supports dry_run to check for conflicts without creating the commit, and custom commit message. Returns: id, short_id, title, author_name, committed_date, web_url. See also: gitlab_commit_get, gitlab_branch_create.

**Parameters:**

- `branch` (string) (required): Target branch name,required
- `dry_run` (boolean): If true, does not create the commit but checks for conflicts
- `message` (string): Custom commit message (defaults to original)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA to cherry-pick

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_comment_create

Post a comment on a commit. Supports file-level inline comments with path and line number.

Returns: note, author, path, and line of the created comment. See also: gitlab_commit_comments.

**Parameters:**

- `line` (integer): Line number to comment on
- `line_type` (string): Line type: new or old (default: new)
- `note` (string) (required): Comment text,required
- `path` (string): File path to comment on (for inline comments)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_comments

List comments on a specific commit. Returns comment text, file path, line number, and author with pagination.

Returns: paginated list of comments with note, path, line, and author. See also: gitlab_commit_get.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_create

Create a Git commit with one or more file actions in a GitLab repository. Each action in the 'actions' array requires: 'action' (create/update/delete/move/chmod), 'file_path', and 'content' (required for create and update). For 'move' actions, also set 'previous_path'. Supports multi-file atomic com...

**Parameters:**

- `actions` () (required): List of file actions (create, update, delete, move),required
- `author_email` (string): Custom author email
- `author_name` (string): Custom author name
- `branch` (string) (required): Target branch name,required
- `commit_message` (string) (required): Commit message,required
- `force` (boolean): When true, force-overwrite the target branch even if a conflict exists
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `start_branch` (string): Branch to start from if target branch does not exist
- `start_sha` (string): SHA to start from if target branch does not exist (alternative to start_branch)

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_diff

List the diffs (changed files) for a specific commit in a GitLab project. Returns old/new paths, diff text, and flags for new/renamed/deleted files with pagination.

Returns: paginated list of diffs with old_path, new_path, diff text, and new/renamed/deleted flags. See also: gitlab_commit_get.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA hash to get diffs for,required
- `unidiff` (boolean): Return diffs in unified diff format

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_get

Retrieve a single commit by SHA from a GitLab project. Returns commit ID, title, full message, author/committer info, parent IDs, stats (additions/deletions/total), and web URL. See also: gitlab_commit_diff, gitlab_commit_merge_requests.

Returns: id, short_id, title, message, author, committer, par...

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA hash to retrieve,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_list

List commits in a GitLab repository. Supports filtering by branch/tag (ref_name), date range (since/until in ISO 8601), file path, and author. Optionally includes commit stats (additions/deletions). Returns commit ID, title, author, date, and web URL with pagination.

Returns: paginated list of comm...

**Parameters:**

- `author` (string): Filter by commit author name or email
- `first_parent` (boolean): Follow only the first parent commit upon seeing a merge commit
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `path` (string): File path to filter commits by (only commits touching this path)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref_name` (string): Branch name, tag, or commit SHA to list commits from (default: default branch)
- `since` (string): Return commits after this date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- `until` (string): Return commits before this date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z)
- `with_stats` (boolean): Include commit stats (additions, deletions, total)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_merge_requests

List merge requests associated with a commit. Returns MR IID, title, state, source/target branches, author, and web URL.

Returns: list of merge requests with iid, title, state, source/target branches, author, and web_url. See also: gitlab_commit_get, gitlab_mr_list.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_refs

Get branches and tags a commit is pushed to. Returns ref type (branch/tag) and name. Supports filtering by type and pagination.

Returns: paginated list of refs with type (branch/tag) and name. See also: gitlab_commit_get, gitlab_branch_list.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA to look up,required
- `type` (string): Filter by ref type: branch, tag, or all (default: all)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_revert

Revert a commit on a target branch. Creates a new commit that undoes the changes of the specified commit. Returns: id, short_id, title, author_name, committed_date, web_url. See also: gitlab_commit_get, gitlab_branch_create.

**Parameters:**

- `branch` (string) (required): Target branch name,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA to revert

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_signature

Get the GPG signature of a commit if it was signed. Returns verification status, key ID, user name, and email.

Returns: verification_status, gpg_key_id, gpg_key_user_name, and gpg_key_user_email. See also: gitlab_commit_get.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_status_set

Set the pipeline status of a commit. State can be: pending, running, success, failed, or canceled. Supports optional ref, name, target_url, description, coverage, and pipeline_id. Returns: id, sha, ref, status, name, description, coverage, pipeline_id. See also: gitlab_commit_statuses.

**Parameters:**

- `context` (string): Status context label (overrides name)
- `coverage` (number): Code coverage percentage
- `description` (string): Short description of the status
- `name` (string): Status name / context
- `pipeline_id` (integer): Pipeline ID to associate the status with
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string): Branch or tag name
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA,required
- `state` (string) (required): Status state: pending, running, success, failed, canceled,required
- `target_url` (string): URL to link from the status

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_commit_statuses

List pipeline statuses of a commit. Returns status state, name, ref, description, and coverage. Supports filtering by ref, stage, name, and pipeline_id with pagination.

Returns: paginated list of statuses with id, state, name, ref, description, and coverage. See also: gitlab_commit_get.

**Parameters:**

- `all` (boolean): Return all statuses including retries
- `name` (string): Status name filter
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `pipeline_id` (integer): Pipeline ID filter
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string): Branch or tag name filter
- `sha` (string) (required): Commit SHA,required
- `stage` (string): Stage name filter

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### confirm (1 tools)

#### gitlab_confirm_vulnerability

Confirm a detected vulnerability (requires Ultimate/Premium). Changes state from DETECTED to CONFIRMED. Returns: updated vulnerability state.

**Parameters:**

- `id` (string) (required): Vulnerability GID (e.g. gid://gitlab/Vulnerability/42),required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

Create a broadcast message. Requires admin access.

Returns: JSON with the created broadcast message details.

See also: gitlab_list_broadcast_messages, gitlab_update_broadcast_message

**Parameters:**

- `broadcast_type` (string): Type: banner or notification
- `dismissable` (): Whether message can be dismissed
- `ends_at` (string): End time in ISO 8601 format
- `font` (string): Font for the message
- `message` (string) (required): Message text. Supports Markdown.,required
- `starts_at` (string): Start time in ISO 8601 format
- `target_access_levels` (): Access levels to target (10=Guest,20=Reporter,30=Developer,40=Maintainer,50=Owner)
- `target_path` (string): Target path to show message on
- `theme` (string): Theme: indigo, light-indigo, blue, light-blue, green, light-green, red, light-red

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_create_custom_emoji

**Parameters:**

- `name` (string) (required): required,Emoji name without colons (e.g. party_parrot)

#### gitlab_create_freeze_period

Returns: JSON with created freeze period including ID, cron schedule, and timezone.

See also: gitlab_list_freeze_periods, gitlab_list_deployments

**Parameters:**

- `cron_timezone` (string): Timezone for cron expressions (e.g. America/New_York)
- `freeze_end` (string) (required): Cron expression for freeze end (e.g. 0 7 * * 1),required

#### gitlab_create_geo_site

Create a new Geo replication site.

Returns: JSON with the created Geo site configuration.

See also: gitlab_list_geo_sites, gitlab_edit_geo_site

**Parameters:**

- `container_repositories_max_capacity` (): Max number of concurrent container repository syncs
- `enabled` (): Whether the site is enabled
- `files_max_capacity` (): Max number of LFS/attachment backfill downloads
- `internal_url` (): Internal URL of the Geo site
- `minimum_reverification_interval` (): Minimum interval (days) before re-verification
- `name` (): Unique name of the Geo site
- `primary` (): Whether this is a primary site
- `repos_max_capacity` (): Max number of concurrent repository backfill syncs
- `selective_sync_namespace_ids` (): Namespace IDs to sync (when selective_sync_type=namespaces)
- `selective_sync_shards` (): Storage shards to sync (when selective_sync_type=shards)
- `selective_sync_type` (): Selective sync type: namespaces or shards
- `sync_object_storage` (): Whether to sync object-stored data
- `url` (): External URL of the Geo site
- `verification_max_capacity` (): Max number of concurrent verification jobs

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_create_mr_context_commits

#### gitlab_create_package_protection_rule

Create a package protection rule for a GitLab project. Restricts push/delete operations on packages matching a name pattern.

Returns: JSON with created rule details. See also: gitlab_list_package_protection_rules.

**Parameters:**

- `minimum_access_level_for_delete` (string): Minimum access level for delete (maintainer, owner, admin)
- `minimum_access_level_for_push` (string): Minimum access level for push (maintainer, owner, admin)
- `package_name_pattern` (string) (required): Package name pattern with optional wildcards (e.g. @my-scope/my-pkg*),required
- `package_type` (string) (required): Package type (npm, pypi, maven, generic, etc.),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

- `name` (string) (required): Name for the secure file,required

#### gitlab_create_user

Create a new GitLab user account (admin only). Requires email, name, and username. Supports setting password, admin status, and profile details.

See also: gitlab_modify_user, gitlab_delete_user

Returns: JSON with created user profile.

**Parameters:**

- `admin` (): Grant admin privileges
- `bio` (string): User bio text
- `email` (string) (required): The user email address,required
- `external` (): Mark user as external
- `force_random_password` (): Set a random password instead of requiring one
- `job_title` (string): User job title
- `location` (string): User location
- `name` (string) (required): The user display name,required
- `note` (string): Admin note about the user
- `organization` (string): User organization
- `password` (string): The user password (required unless reset_password or force_random_password is set)
- `projects_limit` (): Maximum number of projects the user can create
- `reset_password` (): Send a password reset email instead of setting password
- `skip_confirmation` (): Skip confirmation email and activate user immediately
- `username` (string) (required): The username,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_create_user_runner

Create a GitLab CI runner linked to the current user. Runners execute CI/CD jobs. Specify runner_type (instance_type, group_type, or project_type).

See also: gitlab_user_current, gitlab_list_runners

Returns: JSON with runner ID and authentication token.

**Parameters:**

- `access_level` (string): Access level: not_protected or ref_protected
- `description` (string): Runner description
- `group_id` (): Group ID (required for group_type runners)
- `locked` (): Whether the runner should be locked
- `maintenance_note` (string): Maintenance note for the runner
- `maximum_timeout` (): Maximum timeout for jobs in seconds
- `paused` (): Whether the runner should be paused
- `project_id` (): Project ID (required for project_type runners)
- `run_untagged` (): Whether the runner can run untagged jobs
- `runner_type` (string) (required): Runner type: instance_type or group_type or project_type,required
- `tag_list` (): List of runner tags

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_create_work_item

Create a new work item. Requires full_path, work_item_type_id, and title. Supports linked_items to link other work items on creation. Experimental: the Work Items API may introduce breaking changes between minor versions.

Returns: JSON with the created work item details.

See also: gitlab_list_work...

**Parameters:**

- `assignee_ids` (): Global IDs of assignees
- `color` (string): Color hex code (e.g. #fefefe)
- `confidential` (): Whether the work item is confidential
- `description` (string): Description of the work item
- `due_date` (string): Due date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the project or group,required
- `health_status` (string): Health status (onTrack/needsAttention/atRisk)
- `label_ids` (): Global IDs of labels
- `linked_items` (): Linked work items to add on creation
- `milestone_id` (): Global ID of the milestone
- `start_date` (string): Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `title` (string) (required): Title of the work item,required
- `weight` (): Weight of the work item
- `work_item_type_id` (string) (required): Global ID of work item type (e.g. gid://gitlab/WorkItems::Type/1 for Issue),required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_delete_application

Delete an OAuth2 application (admin). Params: id (required).

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_list_applications, gitlab_create_application

**Parameters:**

- `id` (integer) (required): Application ID to delete,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_delete_integration

#### gitlab_delete_user

Delete a GitLab user account (admin only). This permanently removes the user and all their data. Use gitlab_block_user if you want to preserve data.

See also: gitlab_block_user, gitlab_get_user_associations_count

Returns: JSON with deletion confirmation.

**Parameters:**

- `user_id` (integer) (required): The ID of the user to delete,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_delete_user_identity

Delete a user's identity provider link (e.g., LDAP, SAML) from GitLab (admin only). The user account itself is preserved.

See also: gitlab_get_user, gitlab_modify_user

Returns: JSON with deletion confirmation.

**Parameters:**

- `provider` (string) (required): The external provider name (e.g. ldap or saml),required
- `user_id` (integer) (required): The ID of the user,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_delete_work_item

Permanently delete a work item by IID. This action cannot be undone. Experimental: the Work Items API may introduce breaking changes between minor versions.

Returns: JSON confirmation of deletion.

See also: gitlab_list_work_items, gitlab_create_work_item

**Parameters:**

- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the project or group (e.g. my-group/my-project),required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Work item IID,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### deploy (18 tools)

#### gitlab_deploy_key_add

Add a deploy key to a GitLab project with title, public SSH key, and optional push access and expiry date.

See also: gitlab_deploy_key_list_project, gitlab_deploy_key_enable

Returns: JSON with the created deploy key details.

**Parameters:**

- `can_push` (): Whether the key can push to the project
- `expires_at` (string): Expiry date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `key` (string) (required): Public SSH key content,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required
- `title` (string) (required): Deploy key title,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_key_add_instance

Create an instance-level deploy key with title, public SSH key, and optional expiry date. Requires admin access.

See also: gitlab_deploy_key_list_all, gitlab_deploy_key_add

Returns: JSON with the created instance deploy key details.

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): Expiry date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `key` (string) (required): Public SSH key content,required
- `title` (string) (required): Deploy key title,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_key_delete

Remove a deploy key from a GitLab project.

See also: gitlab_deploy_key_list_project, gitlab_deploy_key_add

Returns: confirmation message.

**Parameters:**

- `deploy_key_id` (integer) (required): Deploy key ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_key_enable

Enable an existing deploy key for a project (e.g., a key shared from another project).

See also: gitlab_deploy_key_list_project, gitlab_deploy_key_add

Returns: JSON with the enabled deploy key details.

**Parameters:**

- `deploy_key_id` (integer) (required): Deploy key ID to enable,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_key_get

Get a specific deploy key for a project by its ID.

See also: gitlab_deploy_key_list_project, gitlab_deploy_key_update

Returns: JSON with deploy key details including title, key content, and permissions.

**Parameters:**

- `deploy_key_id` (integer) (required): Deploy key ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_key_list_all

List all instance-level deploy keys. Requires admin access. Filter by public keys.

See also: gitlab_deploy_key_add_instance, gitlab_deploy_key_list_project

Returns: JSON array of instance deploy keys with pagination.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number for pagination
- `per_page` (integer): Results per page (max 100)
- `public` (): Filter by public keys

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_key_list_project

List all deploy keys for a GitLab project.

See also: gitlab_deploy_key_add, gitlab_deploy_token_list_project

Returns: JSON array of deploy keys with pagination.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number for pagination
- `per_page` (integer): Results per page (max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_key_list_user_project

List all deploy keys across projects for a specific user.

See also: gitlab_deploy_key_list_project, gitlab_deploy_key_list_all

Returns: JSON array of deploy keys with pagination.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number for pagination
- `per_page` (integer): Results per page (max 100)
- `user_id` (string) (required): User ID or username,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_key_update

Update an existing deploy key's title or push access permission.

See also: gitlab_deploy_key_get, gitlab_deploy_key_list_project

Returns: JSON with the updated deploy key details.

**Parameters:**

- `can_push` (): Whether the key can push to the project
- `deploy_key_id` (integer) (required): Deploy key ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required
- `title` (string): New deploy key title

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_token_create_group

Create a deploy token for a group with name, scopes, optional username and expiry date.

See also: gitlab_deploy_token_list_group, gitlab_deploy_token_create_project

Returns: JSON with the created deploy token including the token value (shown only once).

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): Expiry date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `name` (string) (required): Deploy token name,required
- `scopes` () (required): Array of scopes (read_repository, read_registry, write_registry, read_package_registry, write_package_registry)
- `username` (string): Username for the deploy token

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_token_create_project

Create a deploy token for a project with name, scopes, optional username and expiry date.

See also: gitlab_deploy_token_list_project, gitlab_deploy_token_create_group

Returns: JSON with the created deploy token including the token value (shown only once).

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): Expiry date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `name` (string) (required): Deploy token name,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `scopes` () (required): Array of scopes (read_repository, read_registry, write_registry, read_package_registry, write_package_registry)
- `username` (string): Username for the deploy token

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_token_delete_group

**Parameters:**

- `deploy_token_id` (integer) (required): Deploy token ID,required

#### gitlab_deploy_token_list_all

List all instance-level deploy tokens. Requires admin access.

See also: gitlab_deploy_token_list_project, gitlab_deploy_token_list_group

Returns: JSON array of deploy tokens with pagination.

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_token_list_group

List all deploy tokens for a GitLab group.

See also: gitlab_deploy_token_create_group, gitlab_deploy_token_list_project

Returns: JSON array of deploy tokens with pagination.

**Parameters:**

- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `page` (integer): Page number for pagination
- `per_page` (integer): Results per page (max 100)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deploy_token_list_project

List all deploy tokens for a GitLab project.

See also: gitlab_deploy_token_create_project, gitlab_deploy_key_list_project

Returns: JSON array of deploy tokens with pagination.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number for pagination
- `per_page` (integer): Results per page (max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### deployment (6 tools)

#### gitlab_deployment_approve_or_reject

Approve or reject a deployment that is blocked waiting for manual approval (environments with required approvals). Set status to 'approved' or 'rejected'. Only works when the deployment is in 'blocked' state â€” use gitlab_deployment_get to check status first. Optionally include a comment.

Returns:...

**Parameters:**

- `comment` (string): Optional comment for the approval or rejection
- `deployment_id` (integer) (required): Deployment ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `status` (string) (required): Approval status: approved or rejected,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deployment_create

Create a new deployment in a GitLab project. Requires environment name, git ref, and SHA. Optionally specify tag flag and initial status. Returns: id, iid, ref, sha, status, user_name, environment_name, created_at. See also: gitlab_deployment_get, gitlab_environment_get.

**Parameters:**

- `environment` (string) (required): Name of the environment to deploy to,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string) (required): Git branch or tag to deploy,required
- `sha` (string) (required): Git SHA to deploy,required
- `status` (string): Deployment status: created or running or success or failed or canceled
- `tag` (): Whether the ref is a tag (default: false)

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deployment_delete

Permanently delete a deployment from a GitLab project. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_deployment_list.

**Parameters:**

- `deployment_id` (integer) (required): Deployment ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deployment_get

Get details of a specific deployment in a GitLab project by its ID. Returns deployment ref, SHA, status, user, environment, and timestamps.

Returns: id, iid, ref, sha, status, user_name, environment_name, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_deployment_list, gitlab_environment_get.

**Parameters:**

- `deployment_id` (integer) (required): Deployment ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deployment_list

List deployments for a GitLab project. Supports filtering by environment name and status (created, running, success, failed, canceled). Returns paginated results with deployment details.

Returns: paginated list of deployments with id, iid, ref, sha, status, environment, and timestamps. See also: gi...

**Parameters:**

- `environment` (string): Filter by environment name
- `order_by` (string): Order by id or iid or created_at or updated_at or finished_at or ref (default: id)
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sort` (string): Sort order: asc or desc (default: asc)
- `status` (string): Filter by status: created or running or success or failed or canceled

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_deployment_update

Update the status of an existing deployment in a GitLab project. Use to transition a deployment between states: created, running, success, failed, canceled. Returns: id, iid, ref, sha, status, user_name, environment_name, updated_at. See also: gitlab_deployment_get.

**Parameters:**

- `deployment_id` (integer) (required): Deployment ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `status` (string) (required): New deployment status: created or running or success or failed or canceled,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### dismiss (1 tools)

#### gitlab_dismiss_vulnerability

Dismiss a vulnerability with optional comment and reason (requires Ultimate/Premium). Valid reasons: ACCEPTABLE_RISK, FALSE_POSITIVE, MITIGATING_CONTROL, USED_IN_TESTS, NOT_APPLICABLE. Returns: updated vulnerability state.

**Parameters:**

- `comment` (string): Reason for dismissal
- `dismissal_reason` (string): Dismissal reason: ACCEPTABLE_RISK, FALSE_POSITIVE, MITIGATING_CONTROL, USED_IN_TESTS, NOT_APPLICABLE
- `id` (string) (required): Vulnerability GID (e.g. gid://gitlab/Vulnerability/42),required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### edit (5 tools)

#### gitlab_edit_geo_site

Update configuration of an existing Geo site.

Returns: JSON with updated Geo site configuration.

See also: gitlab_get_geo_site, gitlab_create_geo_site

**Parameters:**

- `container_repositories_max_capacity` (): Max number of concurrent container repository syncs
- `enabled` (): Whether the site is enabled
- `files_max_capacity` (): Max number of LFS/attachment backfill downloads
- `id` (integer) (required): Numeric ID of the Geo site,required
- `internal_url` (): Internal URL of the Geo site
- `minimum_reverification_interval` (): Minimum interval (days) before re-verification
- `name` (): Unique name of the Geo site
- `repos_max_capacity` (): Max number of concurrent repository backfill syncs
- `selective_sync_namespace_ids` (): Namespace IDs to sync
- `selective_sync_shards` (): Storage shards to sync
- `selective_sync_type` (): Selective sync type: namespaces or shards
- `url` (): External URL of the Geo site
- `verification_max_capacity` (): Max number of concurrent verification jobs

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_edit_project_mirror

Returns: JSON with updated mirror details. See also: gitlab_get_project_mirror.

**Parameters:**

- `auth_method` (string): Authentication method (password or ssh_public_key)
- `enabled` (): Whether the mirror is enabled
- `host_keys` (): SSH host keys for the remote mirror
- `keep_divergent_refs` (): Keep divergent refs on the remote
- `mirror_branch_regex` (string): Regex pattern for branches to mirror
- `mirror_id` (integer) (required): Remote mirror ID,required
- `only_protected_branches` (): Mirror only protected branches
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

### environment (6 tools)

#### gitlab_environment_create

Create a new environment in a GitLab project. Specify name (required), description, external URL, and tier (production, staging, testing, development, other). Returns: id, name, slug, state, tier, external_url, created_at. See also: gitlab_environment_get.

**Parameters:**

- `description` (string): Description of the environment
- `external_url` (string): URL of the environment's external deployment
- `name` (string) (required): Environment name (e.g. production, staging),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `tier` (string): Deployment tier: production, staging, testing, development, other

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_environment_delete

Permanently delete an environment from a GitLab project. The environment must be stopped before it can be deleted.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_environment_list.

**Parameters:**

- `environment_id` (integer) (required): Environment ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_environment_get

Get details of a specific environment in a GitLab project by its ID. Returns environment name, state, tier, external URL, and timestamps.

Returns: id, name, slug, state, tier, external_url, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_environment_update, gitlab_deployment_list.

**Parameters:**

- `environment_id` (integer) (required): Environment ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_environment_list

List environments for a GitLab project. Supports filtering by name, search term, and state. Returns paginated results with environment details including tier, state, and external URL.

Returns: paginated list of environments with id, name, slug, state, tier, and external_url. See also: gitlab_enviro...

**Parameters:**

- `name` (string): Filter by exact environment name
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `search` (string): Search environments by name (fuzzy)
- `states` (string): Filter by state: available, stopping, stopped

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_environment_stop

Stop a running environment in a GitLab project. Triggers any on_stop actions defined in CI/CD. Use force=true to stop even if the environment has active deployments. Returns: id, name, slug, state, tier, external_url. See also: gitlab_environment_get.

**Parameters:**

- `environment_id` (integer) (required): Environment ID,required
- `force` (): Force stop even if environment has active deployments
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_environment_update

Update an existing environment in a GitLab project. Can modify name, description, external URL, and tier. Returns: id, name, slug, state, tier, external_url, updated_at. See also: gitlab_environment_get.

**Parameters:**

- `description` (string): Updated description
- `environment_id` (integer) (required): Environment ID,required
- `external_url` (string): Updated external URL
- `name` (string): New environment name
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `tier` (string): Updated tier: production, staging, testing, development, other

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### epic (15 tools)

#### gitlab_epic_create

Create a new epic in a GitLab group (via Work Items GraphQL API). Supports title, description, confidentiality, color, dates, assignees, labels, weight, and health status.

Returns: JSON with created epic details. See also: gitlab_epic_get.

**Parameters:**

- `assignee_ids` (): Global IDs of assignees
- `color` (string): Epic color (hex format, e.g. #FF0000)
- `confidential` (): Whether the epic is confidential
- `description` (string): Epic description (Markdown supported)
- `due_date` (string): Due date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required
- `health_status` (string): Health status (onTrack/needsAttention/atRisk)
- `label_ids` (): Global IDs of labels
- `start_date` (string): Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `title` (string) (required): Epic title,required
- `weight` (): Weight of the epic

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_delete

Permanently delete an epic from a GitLab group (via Work Items GraphQL API).

Returns: JSON with deletion confirmation. See also: gitlab_epic_get.

**Parameters:**

- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_get

Get a single group epic by its IID (via Work Items GraphQL API). Returns title, description, state, labels, dates, author, assignees, linked items, and health status.

Returns: JSON with epic details. See also: gitlab_epic_list, gitlab_epic_get_links.

**Parameters:**

- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_get_links

Get all child epics of a parent epic (via REST API). Returns the list of sub-epics linked to the specified epic.

Note: This tool uses the REST API because the Work Items GraphQL API does not yet support listing children.

Returns: JSON with child_epics array. See also: gitlab_epic_get.

**Parameters:**

- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_issue_assign

Assign an existing issue to a GitLab group epic via the Work Items GraphQL API. The issue is identified by its project path and IID.

Returns: JSON with the epic and child work item GIDs. See also: gitlab_epic_issue_list.

**Parameters:**

- `child_iid` (integer) (required): IID of the issue to assign to the epic,required
- `child_project_path` (string) (required): Full project path of the issue to assign (e.g. my-group/my-project),required
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group that contains the epic,required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_issue_list

List all child issues of a GitLab group epic via the Work Items GraphQL API. Supports cursor-based pagination.

Returns: JSON with issues array and pagination metadata. See also: gitlab_epic_get.

**Parameters:**

- `after` (string): Cursor for forward pagination (from previous response end_cursor)
- `before` (string): Cursor for backward pagination (from previous response start_cursor)
- `first` (): Number of items to return (default 20, max 100)
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group or my-group/sub-group),required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required
- `last` (): Number of items from the end (backward pagination)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_issue_remove

Remove an issue from a GitLab group epic via the Work Items GraphQL API. Clears the child's parent reference.

Returns: JSON with the epic and child work item GIDs. See also: gitlab_epic_issue_list.

**Parameters:**

- `child_iid` (integer) (required): IID of the issue to remove from the epic,required
- `child_project_path` (string) (required): Full project path of the issue to remove,required
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group that contains the epic,required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_issue_update

Reorder an issue within a GitLab group epic via the Work Items GraphQL API. Moves the issue before or after a reference issue.

Returns: JSON with the updated issues list. See also: gitlab_epic_issue_list.

**Parameters:**

- `adjacent_id` (string): Work item GID of the reference issue to position relative to
- `child_id` (string) (required): Work item GID of the issue to reorder (from list output id field),required
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group that contains the epic,required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required
- `relative_position` (string): Position relative to adjacent item: BEFORE or AFTER

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_list

List epics for a GitLab group (via Work Items GraphQL API with type=Epic). Supports filtering by state, labels, author, search text, and cursor-based pagination.

Returns: JSON with epics array. See also: gitlab_epic_get.

**Parameters:**

- `after` (string): Cursor for forward pagination
- `author_username` (string): Filter by author username
- `confidential` (): Filter by confidentiality
- `first` (): Number of items to return (cursor-based pagination)
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group or my-group/sub-group),required
- `include_ancestors` (): Include epics from ancestor groups
- `include_descendants` (): Include epics from descendant groups
- `label_name` (): Filter by label names
- `search` (string): Search in title and description
- `sort` (string): Sort order
- `state` (string): Filter by state (opened/closed/all)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_note_create

Add a comment (note) to a GitLab group epic via the Work Items GraphQL API. Supports Markdown formatting.

Returns: JSON with created note including ID, body, author, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_epic_get.

**Parameters:**

- `body` (string) (required): Note body (Markdown supported),required
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_note_delete

Permanently delete a comment from a GitLab group epic via the Work Items GraphQL API. Only the note author or a group maintainer can delete a note.

Returns: JSON with deletion confirmation. See also: gitlab_epic_note_get.

**Parameters:**

- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): ID of the note to delete,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_note_get

Get a single comment (note) from a GitLab group epic by its note ID via the Work Items GraphQL API, including author, timestamps, body, and system flag.

Returns: JSON with note details including ID, body, author, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_epic_note_list.

**Parameters:**

- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): ID of the note to retrieve,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_note_list

List all comments (notes) on a GitLab group epic via the Work Items GraphQL API. Supports cursor-based pagination.

Returns: JSON with notes array including body, author, timestamps, and system flags. See also: gitlab_epic_get.

**Parameters:**

- `after` (string): Cursor for forward pagination (from previous response end_cursor)
- `before` (string): Cursor for backward pagination (from previous response start_cursor)
- `first` (): Number of items to return (default 20, max 100)
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group or my-group/sub-group),required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required
- `last` (): Number of items from the end (backward pagination)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_note_update

Edit the body text of an existing comment on a GitLab group epic via the Work Items GraphQL API. Only the note author or a group maintainer can update a note.

Returns: JSON with updated note including ID, body, author, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_epic_note_get.

**Parameters:**

- `body` (string) (required): Updated note body (Markdown supported),required
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): ID of the note to update,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_epic_update

Update an existing group epic (via Work Items GraphQL API). Can modify title, description, labels, state (CLOSE/REOPEN), confidentiality, parent, color, dates, assignees, weight, health status, and status.

Returns: JSON with updated epic details. See also: gitlab_epic_get.

**Parameters:**

- `add_label_ids` (): Global IDs of labels to add
- `assignee_ids` (): Global IDs of assignees (empty array to remove all)
- `color` (string): Epic color (hex format)
- `description` (string): Updated description (Markdown supported)
- `due_date` (string): Due date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `full_path` (string) (required): Full path of the group (e.g. my-group),required
- `health_status` (string): Health status (onTrack/needsAttention/atRisk)
- `iid` (integer) (required): Epic IID within the group,required
- `parent_id` (): Global ID of the parent epic work item
- `remove_label_ids` (): Global IDs of labels to remove
- `start_date` (string): Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `state_event` (string): State event: CLOSE or REOPEN
- `status` (string): Work item status: TODO, IN_PROGRESS, DONE, WONT_DO, or DUPLICATE
- `title` (string): Updated epic title
- `weight` (): Weight of the epic

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### feature (5 tools)

#### gitlab_feature_flag_create

Create a new feature flag for a project.

Returns: JSON with created feature flag including name, active status, and strategies.

See also: gitlab_feature_flag_list, gitlab_ff_user_list_create

**Parameters:**

- `active` (): Whether the flag is active
- `description` (string): Feature flag description
- `name` (string) (required): Feature flag name,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required
- `strategies` (string): JSON array of strategy objects: [{name, parameters, scopes}]
- `version` (string): Version of the feature flag (new_version_flag)

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_feature_flag_delete

Delete a feature flag.

Returns: JSON with deletion confirmation.

See also: gitlab_feature_flag_list, gitlab_feature_flag_create

**Parameters:**

- `name` (string) (required): Feature flag name,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_feature_flag_get

Get a single feature flag by name.

Returns: JSON with feature flag details including name, active status, and strategies.

See also: gitlab_feature_flag_list, gitlab_feature_flag_update

**Parameters:**

- `name` (string) (required): Feature flag name,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_feature_flag_list

List feature flags for a project.

Returns: JSON with feature flags array including name, active status, and strategies.

See also: gitlab_feature_flag_get, gitlab_ff_user_list_list

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required
- `scope` (string): Filter by scope (enabled or disabled)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_feature_flag_update

Update an existing feature flag.

Returns: JSON with updated feature flag including name, active status, and strategies.

See also: gitlab_feature_flag_get, gitlab_feature_flag_delete

**Parameters:**

- `active` (): Whether the flag is active
- `description` (string): Feature flag description
- `name` (string) (required): Current feature flag name,required
- `new_name` (string): New feature flag name
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or path,required
- `strategies` (string): JSON array of strategy objects: [{id, name, parameters, scopes}]

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### file (8 tools)

#### gitlab_file_blame

Get blame information for a file in a GitLab repository. Shows which commit and author last modified each line range. Supports optional line range filtering. Returns: file_path and blame ranges with commit ID, author, message, and lines. See also: gitlab_file_get, gitlab_commit_get.

**Parameters:**

- `file_path` (string) (required): URL-encoded full path of the file,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `range_end` (integer): End line number for blame range
- `range_start` (integer): Start line number for blame range
- `ref` (string): Branch, tag, or commit SHA (defaults to default branch)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_file_create

Create a new file in a GitLab repository. Requires branch and commit message. Optionally specify encoding (text/base64), start_branch, and author info. Returns: file_path and branch. See also: gitlab_commit_create, gitlab_file_get.

**Parameters:**

- `author_email` (string): Commit author email
- `author_name` (string): Commit author name
- `branch` (string) (required): Branch to create the file on,required
- `commit_message` (string) (required): Commit message,required
- `content` (string) (required): File content,required
- `encoding` (string): Content encoding: text or base64 (default: text)
- `execute_filemode` (): Enable execute permission on the file
- `file_path` (string) (required): URL-encoded full path of the new file,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `start_branch` (string): Branch to start from (creates new branch if different from branch)

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_file_delete

Delete a file from a GitLab repository. Requires branch and commit message. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_file_get.

**Parameters:**

- `author_email` (string): Commit author email
- `author_name` (string): Commit author name
- `branch` (string) (required): Branch to delete the file from,required
- `commit_message` (string) (required): Commit message,required
- `file_path` (string) (required): URL-encoded full path of the file to delete,required
- `last_commit_id` (string): Last known commit ID for optimistic locking
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `start_branch` (string): Branch to start from

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_file_get

Retrieve the decoded content of a single file from a GitLab repository at a specific ref (branch name, tag name, or commit SHA). Returns file content, size, encoding, and last commit ID. This is the primary tool for reading file contents. For raw text without metadata use gitlab_file_raw. For metada...

**Parameters:**

- `file_path` (string) (required): URL-encoded full path of the file (e.g. src%2Fmain.go or src/main.go),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string): Branch name, tag, or commit SHA (defaults to default branch)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_file_metadata

Get file metadata WITHOUT retrieving content. Use this to check file existence or properties; for content use gitlab_file_get. Returns: file_name, size, encoding, blob_id, commit_id, last_commit_id, and SHA-256.

See also: gitlab_file_get.

**Parameters:**

- `file_path` (string) (required): URL-encoded full path of the file,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string): Branch, tag, or commit SHA (defaults to default branch)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_file_raw

Get the raw content of a file from a GitLab repository as plain text without metadata. Use gitlab_file_get if you also need metadata. Returns: file_path, size, and content as plain text. See also: gitlab_file_get.

**Parameters:**

- `file_path` (string) (required): URL-encoded full path of the file,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string): Branch, tag, or commit SHA (defaults to default branch)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_file_raw_metadata

Get file metadata via HEAD request to the raw file endpoint. Returns size, encoding, blob ID, commit IDs, and SHA-256 without retrieving content. Lighter than gitlab_file_metadata â€” uses a HEAD request instead of GET.

Returns: file_name, size, encoding, blob_id, commit_id, last_commit_id, and SHA...

**Parameters:**

- `file_path` (string) (required): URL-encoded full path of the file,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string): Branch, tag, or commit SHA (defaults to default branch)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_file_update

Update an existing file in a GitLab repository. Requires branch and commit message. Supports last_commit_id for optimistic locking. Returns: file_path and branch. See also: gitlab_file_get.

**Parameters:**

- `author_email` (string): Commit author email
- `author_name` (string): Commit author name
- `branch` (string) (required): Branch to update the file on,required
- `commit_message` (string) (required): Commit message,required
- `content` (string) (required): New file content,required
- `encoding` (string): Content encoding: text or base64 (default: text)
- `execute_filemode` (): Enable execute permission on the file
- `file_path` (string) (required): URL-encoded full path of the file to update,required
- `last_commit_id` (string): Last known commit ID for optimistic locking
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `start_branch` (string): Branch to start from

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

Requires the MCP client to support the samp...

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string) (required): Branch or tag to search in (defaults to default branch)

Requires the MCP client to support the sampli...

#### gitlab_get_group_dora_metrics

Get DORA metrics for a GitLab group. Requires metric type (deployment_frequency, lead_time_for_changes, time_to_restore_service, change_failure_rate). Supports date range and interval filtering. Returns: list of date/value data points.

**Parameters:**

- `end_date` (string): End date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `environment_tiers` (): Filter by environment tiers (e.g. production, staging)
- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `interval` (string): Aggregation interval: daily, monthly, all (default: daily)
- `metric` (string) (required): DORA metric type: deployment_frequency, lead_time_for_changes, time_to_restore_service, change_failure_rate,required
- `start_date` (string): Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

- `event_id` (integer) (required): Audit event ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

- `slug` (string) (required): Integration slug (e.g. jira, slack, discord, mattermost, microsoft-teams, telegram, emails-on-push, pipelines-email, ext...

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

- `search` (string) (required): Search string

#### gitlab_get_project_dora_metrics

Get DORA metrics for a GitLab project. Requires metric type (deployment_frequency, lead_time_for_changes, time_to_restore_service, change_failure_rate). Supports date range and interval filtering. Returns: list of date/value data points.

**Parameters:**

- `end_date` (string): End date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `environment_tiers` (): Filter by environment tiers (e.g. production, staging)
- `interval` (string): Aggregation interval: daily, monthly, all (default: daily)
- `metric` (string) (required): DORA metric type: deployment_frequency, lead_time_for_changes, time_to_restore_service, change_failure_rate,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `start_date` (string): Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

Returns: JSON array of user activities with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of memberships with pagination.

### group (95 tools)

#### gitlab_group_access_token_create

Create a new group access token with specified name, scopes, access level, and optional expiry date.

See also: gitlab_group_access_token_list, gitlab_project_access_token_create

Returns: JSON with the created access token including the token value.

**Parameters:**

- `access_level` (integer): Access level: 10 (guest), 20 (reporter), 30 (developer), 40 (maintainer), 50 (owner)
- `description` (string): Token description
- `expires_at` (string): Expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `name` (string) (required): Token name,required
- `scopes` () (required): Token scopes: api, read_api, read_repository, write_repository, etc.,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_group_access_token_get

Get a specific group access token by its ID.

See also: gitlab_group_access_token_list, gitlab_group_access_token_rotate

Returns: JSON with access token details.

**Parameters:**

- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `token_id` (integer) (required): Access token ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_group_access_token_list

List all access tokens for a GitLab group. Filter by state (active, inactive).

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_list, gitlab_personal_access_token_list

Returns: JSON array of access tokens with pagination.

**Parameters:**

- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `state` (string): Token state filter: active, inactive

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_group_access_token_revoke

Revoke a group access token. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_group_access_token_list, gitlab_group_access_token_create

Returns: confirmation message.

**Parameters:**

- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `token_id` (integer) (required): Access token ID to revoke,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_group_access_token_rotate

Rotate a group access token, generating a new token value. Optionally set a new expiry date.

See also: gitlab_group_access_token_list, gitlab_group_access_token_revoke

Returns: JSON with the rotated access token including the new token value.

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): New expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `token_id` (integer) (required): Access token ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_group_access_token_rotate_self

Rotate the group access token used for the current request. Returns the new token value.

See also: gitlab_group_access_token_rotate, gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate_self

Returns: JSON with the rotated access token including the new token value.

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): New expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_group_archive

Archive a GitLab group. Requires Owner role or administrator.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_group_unarchive, gitlab_group_get.

**Parameters:**

- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_group_board_create

Create a new issue board in a group

See also: gitlab_group_board_list, gitlab_group_board_list_create

Returns: JSON with the board details.

**Parameters:**

- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or path,required
- `name` (string) (required): Board name,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_group_board_delete

Delete a group issue board. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_group_board_list, gitlab_group_board_create

Returns: confirmation message.

**Parameters:**

- `board_id` (integer) (required): Board ID,required
- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or path,required

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)

#### gitlab_group_board_update

Update an existing group issue board

See also: gitlab_group_board_get, gitlab_group_board_list

Returns: JSON with the board details.

**Parameters:**

- `assignee_id` (integer): Assignee user ID
- `board_id` (integer) (required): Board ID,required
