#### gitlab_mr_delete

Permanently delete a GitLab merge request. This action cannot be undone. Requires at least Maintainer access level.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_list.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID to delete (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_dependencies_list

List all merge request dependencies (blockers) for a GitLab merge request. Returns the list of MRs that must be merged before this MR can be merged. Requires Premium or Ultimate license.

Returns: JSON array of blocking merge requests with iid, title, state, and web_url. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_dependency_create

Create a merge request dependency (blocker). The specified blocking MR must be merged before this MR can be merged. Requires Premium or Ultimate license.

Returns: JSON with the dependency details including blocking merge request information. See also: gitlab_mr_dependencies_list.

**Parameters:**

- `blocking_merge_request_id` (integer) (required): ID of the merge request that blocks this one
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_dependency_delete

Remove a merge request dependency (blocker). The specified blocking MR will no longer prevent this MR from being merged. Requires Premium or Ultimate license.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_dependencies_list.

**Parameters:**

- `blocking_merge_request_id` (integer) (required): ID of the blocking merge request to remove
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_diff_version_get

Get a single merge request diff version with its commits and file diffs. Use the version_id from gitlab_mr_diff_versions_list. Optionally request unified diff format.

Returns: JSON with diff version commits and file diffs. See also: gitlab_mr_diff_versions_list.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `unidiff` (boolean): Return diffs in unified diff format (default: false)
- `version_id` (integer) (required): Diff version ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_diff_versions_list

List all diff versions (historical snapshots) of a merge request. Returns version IDs, SHAs, state, and timestamps â€” NOT the actual diffs. Use gitlab_mr_diff_version_get with a version_id to retrieve diffs for a specific version. For current diffs, use gitlab_mr_changes_get instead.

Returns: JSON...

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_discussion_create

Start a new threaded discussion on a GitLab merge request. For a general discussion, just provide 'body'. For an inline diff comment, also provide 'position' with base_sha, start_sha, head_sha (get these from gitlab_mr_get diff_refs field), new_path, and old_line/new_line.

Returns: JSON with the cr...

**Parameters:**

- `body` (string) (required): Discussion body,required
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `position` (): Diff position for inline comments. Omit for general MR discussions.
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_discussion_get

Get a single discussion thread from a GitLab merge request by its discussion ID, including all notes in the thread.

Returns: JSON with discussion details and notes. See also: gitlab_mr_discussion_list.

**Parameters:**

- `discussion_id` (string) (required): ID of the discussion,required
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_discussion_list

List all discussion threads on a GitLab merge request including inline diff comments and general discussions. Each thread contains its notes and resolution status. Returns paginated results.

Returns: JSON array of discussions with pagination. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_note_list.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_discussion_note_delete

Delete a note from a merge request discussion thread. Only the note author or project maintainers can delete notes.

Returns: JSON confirming note deletion. See also: gitlab_mr_discussion_get.

**Parameters:**

- `discussion_id` (string) (required): ID of the discussion containing the note,required
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): ID of the note to delete,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_discussion_note_update

Update the body or resolved status of a note within a merge request discussion thread. You can modify the text, change resolution status, or both.

Returns: JSON with the updated note details. See also: gitlab_mr_discussion_get.

**Parameters:**

- `body` (string): New body text (Markdown). Leave empty to keep current body.
- `discussion_id` (string) (required): ID of the discussion containing the note,required
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): ID of the note to update,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `resolved` (): Set to true to resolve, false to unresolve. Omit to leave unchanged.

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_discussion_reply

Add a reply to an existing discussion thread on a GitLab merge request. The reply appears nested under the original discussion note.

Returns: JSON with the created reply note. See also: gitlab_mr_discussion_get.

**Parameters:**

- `body` (string) (required): Reply body,required
- `discussion_id` (string) (required): ID of the discussion to reply to,required
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_discussion_resolve

Resolve or unresolve a discussion thread on a GitLab merge request. Resolved discussions are collapsed in the UI to reduce review noise.

Returns: JSON with the updated discussion resolution status. See also: gitlab_mr_discussion_get.

**Parameters:**

- `discussion_id` (string) (required): ID of the discussion to resolve,required
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `resolved` (boolean) (required): True to resolve, false to unresolve,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_draft_note_create

Create a new draft note (pending review comment) on a GitLab merge request. Supports inline diff comments on specific lines via the position parameter. Draft notes stay private until published with draft_note_publish_all. Prefer this over discussion_create for code reviews to batch all comments into...

**Parameters:**

- `commit_id` (string): SHA of the commit to comment on
- `in_reply_to_discussion_id` (string): Discussion ID to reply to
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `note` (string) (required): Note body text (Markdown),required
- `position` (): Diff position for inline comments on specific lines. Omit for general MR comments.
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `resolve_discussion` (): Resolve the discussion when published

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_draft_note_delete

Permanently delete a draft note from a GitLab merge request. Only the draft author can delete it.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_draft_note_list.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): Draft note ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_draft_note_get

Get a single draft note from a GitLab merge request by its ID. Returns the note body, author, and associated commit/discussion details.

Returns: JSON with draft note details. See also: gitlab_mr_draft_note_list.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): Draft note ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_draft_note_list

List all draft notes (pending review comments) on a GitLab merge request. Draft notes are only visible to the author until published. Supports pagination and sorting.

Returns: JSON array of draft notes. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_discussion_list.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `order_by` (string): Order by: id (default)
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sort` (string): Sort: asc or desc (default)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_draft_note_publish

Publish a single draft note on a GitLab merge request, making it visible to all participants. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_draft_note_get.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): Draft note ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_draft_note_publish_all

Publish all pending draft notes on a GitLab merge request at once, making them visible to all participants. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_draft_note_list.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_draft_note_update

Update the body text of an existing draft note on a GitLab merge request. Only the draft author can update it.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `note` (string): Updated note body text (Markdown)
- `note_id` (integer) (required): Draft note ID,required
- `position` (): Updated diff position for inline comments
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_list

List merge requests in a GitLab project. Supports filtering by state (opened/closed/merged/all), author, assignee, reviewer, labels, milestone, and source/target branch. Returns paginated results.

Returns: JSON array of merge requests with pagination. Fields include iid, title, state, author, sourc...

**Parameters:**

- `author_username` (string): Filter by author username
- `created_after` (string): Return MRs created after date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- `created_before` (string): Return MRs created before date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z)
- `draft` (): Filter by draft status (true=only drafts, false=only non-drafts)
- `iids` (): Filter by merge request internal IDs
- `labels` (string): Comma-separated label names to filter by
- `milestone` (string): Milestone title to filter by
- `not_labels` (string): Comma-separated label names to exclude
- `order_by` (string): Order by field (created_at, updated_at, title)
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `scope` (string): Filter by scope (created_by_me, assigned_to_me, all)
- `search` (string): Search in title and description
- `sort` (string): Sort direction (asc, desc)
- `source_branch` (string): Filter by source branch name
- `state` (string): Filter by state (opened, closed, merged, all)
- `target_branch` (string): Filter by target branch name
- `updated_after` (string): Return MRs updated after date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- `updated_before` (string): Return MRs updated before date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_list_global

List merge requests across all projects visible to the authenticated user. Supports filtering by state (opened/closed/merged/all), author, reviewer, labels, milestone, draft status, and date ranges. Returns paginated results.

Returns: JSON array of merge requests with pagination. Fields include iid...

**Parameters:**

- `author_username` (string): Filter by author username
- `created_after` (string): Return MRs created after date (ISO 8601)
- `created_before` (string): Return MRs created before date (ISO 8601)
- `draft` (): Filter by draft status (true=only drafts, false=only non-drafts)
- `labels` (string): Comma-separated label names to filter by
- `milestone` (string): Milestone title to filter by
- `not_labels` (string): Comma-separated label names to exclude
- `order_by` (string): Order by field (created_at, updated_at)
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `reviewer_username` (string): Filter by reviewer username
- `scope` (string): Filter by scope (created_by_me, assigned_to_me, all)
- `search` (string): Search in title and description
- `sort` (string): Sort direction (asc, desc)
- `source_branch` (string): Filter by source branch name
- `state` (string): Filter by state (opened, closed, merged, all)
- `target_branch` (string): Filter by target branch name
- `updated_after` (string): Return MRs updated after date (ISO 8601)
- `updated_before` (string): Return MRs updated before date (ISO 8601)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

List merge requests in a GitLab group. Supports filtering by state (opened/closed/merged/all), author, reviewer, labels, milestone, draft status, and date ranges. Returns paginated results.

Returns: JSON array of merge requests with pagination. Fields include iid, title, state, author, source/targe...

**Parameters:**

- `author_username` (string): Filter by author username
- `created_after` (string): Return MRs created after date (ISO 8601)
- `created_before` (string): Return MRs created before date (ISO 8601)
- `draft` (): Filter by draft status (true=only drafts, false=only non-drafts)
- `group_id` (string) (required): Group ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `labels` (string): Comma-separated label names to filter by
- `milestone` (string): Milestone title to filter by
- `not_labels` (string): Comma-separated label names to exclude
- `order_by` (string): Order by field (created_at, updated_at)
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `reviewer_username` (string): Filter by reviewer username
- `scope` (string): Filter by scope (created_by_me, assigned_to_me, all)
- `search` (string): Search in title and description
- `sort` (string): Sort direction (asc, desc)
- `source_branch` (string): Filter by source branch name
- `state` (string): Filter by state (opened, closed, merged, all)
- `target_branch` (string): Filter by target branch name
- `updated_after` (string): Return MRs updated after date (ISO 8601)
- `updated_before` (string): Return MRs updated before date (ISO 8601)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_merge

Merge an accepted GitLab merge request into its target branch. Supports optional squash commits, custom merge commit message, and automatic source branch deletion after merge. The server automatically detects enforced project settings (squash_on_merge, force_remove_source_branch) and applies them â€...

**Parameters:**

- `auto_merge` (): Automatically merge when pipeline succeeds (auto-merge)
- `merge_commit_message` (string): Custom merge commit message
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sha` (string): Head SHA of the merge request â€” merge only if HEAD matches (safety check)
- `should_remove_source_branch` (): Delete source branch after merge. Only set if explicitly requested by the user. Omit to preserve repository defaults
- `squash` (): Squash commits on merge. Only set if explicitly requested by the user. Omit to preserve repository defaults
- `squash_commit_message` (string): Custom squash commit message (used when squash is enabled)

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

Get a single milestone event for a merge request.

Returns: JSON with milestone event details including id, action, user, created_at.

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_note_create

Add a comment (note) to a GitLab merge request. The comment appears in the merge request's activity timeline as a top-level note.

**Parameters:**

- `body` (string) (required): Comment body (Markdown supported),required
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

List all award emoji on a merge request note.

See also: gitlab_mr_note_emoji_create, gitlab_mr_emoji_list

Returns: JSON array of award emoji with pagination. Fields include id, name, user, and awardable_type.

**Parameters:**

- `iid` (integer) (required): Issue IID or MR IID or Snippet ID,required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): Note ID,required
- `page` (integer): Page number for pagination (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Number of items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_note_get

Get a single comment (note) from a GitLab merge request by its note ID, including author, timestamps, resolution status, and body.

Returns: JSON with note details (ID, author, body, timestamps, resolution status). See also: gitlab_mr_notes_list.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): ID of the note to retrieve,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_note_update

Edit the body text of an existing comment on a GitLab merge request. Only the note author or a project maintainer can update a note.

Returns: JSON with the updated note details. See also: gitlab_mr_note_get.

**Parameters:**

- `body` (string) (required): Updated comment body,required
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `note_id` (integer) (required): ID of the note to update,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_notes_list

List all comments (notes) on a GitLab merge request ordered by creation date. Includes both user comments and system-generated notes (status changes, label updates).

Returns: JSON with array of notes and pagination info. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_discussion_list.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `order_by` (string): Order by field (created_at, updated_at)
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sort` (string): Sort direction (asc, desc)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_participants

List all participants (users who have interacted via comments, approvals, or commits) in a GitLab merge request. This includes anyone who participated, not just assigned reviewers. For assigned reviewers with review state, use gitlab_mr_reviewers instead.

Returns: JSON array of participant users wi...

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_pipelines

List all pipelines associated with a GitLab merge request. Returns pipeline ID, status, source, ref, SHA, and web URL.

Returns: JSON array of pipelines with pagination. Fields include id, status, source, ref, sha, and web_url. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_pipeline_get.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_raw_diffs

Get the raw unified-diff output for a merge request in plain-text git-apply format. Use this for programmatic diff analysis or patch application. For human-readable structured diffs, use gitlab_mr_changes_get instead.

Returns: plain-text unified diff output.

See also: gitlab_mr_changes_get.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request internal ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_rebase

Rebase a merge request's source branch against its target branch. Optionally skip triggering CI pipeline after rebase. Returns whether the rebase is in progress.

Returns: JSON with rebase_in_progress status. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID to rebase (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `skip_ci` (boolean): Skip triggering CI pipeline after rebase

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

#### gitlab_mr_set_time_estimate

Set the time estimate for a GitLab merge request using a human-readable duration string (e.g. '3h30m', '1w2d'). Returns updated time stats.

Returns: JSON with time stats including time_estimate, total_time_spent, and human-readable formats. See also: gitlab_mr_time_stats.

- `duration` (string) (required): Human-readable duration (e.g. 3h30m, 1w2d),required
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_subscribe

Subscribe to a GitLab merge request to receive notifications. Returns the updated MR. Returns 304 if already subscribed.

Returns: JSON with the updated merge request details. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_time_stats

Get time tracking statistics for a GitLab merge request including estimated time and total time spent in both human-readable and seconds format.

Returns: JSON with time stats including time_estimate, total_time_spent, and human-readable formats. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_unapprove

Remove the authenticated user's approval from a GitLab merge request.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_mr_get, gitlab_mr_approve.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_unsubscribe

Unsubscribe from a GitLab merge request to stop receiving notifications. Returns the updated MR. Returns 304 if not subscribed.

Returns: JSON with the updated merge request details. See also: gitlab_mr_get.

**Parameters:**

- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_mr_update

Update a GitLab merge request's title, description, target branch, assignees, reviewers, labels (replace, add, or remove), milestone, discussion_locked, allow_collaboration, or state event (close/reopen). The squash and remove_source_branch options are omitted by default to preserve repository-level...

**Parameters:**

- `add_labels` (string): Comma-separated label names to add without removing existing
- `allow_collaboration` (): Allow commits from upstream members who can merge to target branch
- `assignee_id` (integer): Single user ID to assign (use assignee_ids for multiple)
- `assignee_ids` (): User IDs to assign as merge request assignees
- `description` (string): New description
- `discussion_locked` (): Lock discussions on the merge request
- `labels` (string): Comma-separated label names to replace all labels on the merge request
- `milestone_id` (integer): Milestone ID (0 to unset)
- `mr_iid` (integer) (required): Merge request IID (project-scoped, not 'merge_request_id'),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `remove_labels` (string): Comma-separated label names to remove
- `remove_source_branch` (): Delete source branch after merge. Only set if explicitly requested
- `reviewer_ids` (): User IDs to add as reviewers
- `squash` (): Squash commits on merge. Only set if explicitly requested
- `state_event` (string): State transition (close, reopen)
- `target_branch` (string): New target branch
- `title` (string): New title

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### namespace (4 tools)

#### gitlab_namespace_exists

Check whether a namespace path exists (is taken). Returns availability and suggested alternatives if the path is taken.

Returns: JSON with namespace availability status.

See also: gitlab_namespace_get, gitlab_namespace_search

**Parameters:**

- `id` (string) (required): Namespace path to check for existence,required
- `parent_id` (integer): Parent namespace ID to scope the check

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_namespace_get

Get details of a single namespace by ID or path.

Returns: JSON with namespace details.

See also: gitlab_namespace_list, gitlab_namespace_search

**Parameters:**

- `id` (string) (required): Namespace ID or path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_namespace_list

List all namespaces visible to the authenticated user. Supports filtering by search, owned-only, top-level-only, and pagination.

Returns: JSON array of namespaces with pagination.

See also: gitlab_namespace_get, gitlab_list_groups

**Parameters:**

- `owned_only` (boolean): If true return only namespaces owned by the authenticated user
- `page` (integer): Page number for pagination (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Number of items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `search` (string): Filter namespaces by search term
- `top_level_only` (boolean): If true return only top-level namespaces

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_namespace_search

Search namespaces by query string. Returns matching namespaces with pagination.

Returns: JSON array of matching namespaces with pagination.

See also: gitlab_namespace_list, gitlab_namespace_exists

**Parameters:**

- `query` (string) (required): Search query string for namespaces,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### notification (6 tools)

#### gitlab_notification_global_get

Get global notification settings for the authenticated user.

Returns: JSON with global notification settings.

See also: gitlab_notification_global_update, gitlab_notification_project_get

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_notification_global_update

Update global notification settings for the authenticated user.

Returns: JSON with the updated global notification settings.

See also: gitlab_notification_global_get, gitlab_notification_project_update

**Parameters:**

- `close_issue` (): Notify on issue close
- `close_merge_request` (): Notify on MR close
- `failed_pipeline` (): Notify on pipeline failure
- `fixed_pipeline` (): Notify on pipeline fix
- `issue_due` (): Notify on issue due date
- `level` (string): Notification level: disabled, participating, watch, global, mention, custom
- `merge_merge_request` (): Notify on MR merge
- `merge_when_pipeline_succeeds` (): Notify on merge when pipeline succeeds
- `moved_project` (): Notify on project move
- `new_epic` (): Notify on new epic
- `new_issue` (): Notify on new issue
- `new_merge_request` (): Notify on new MR
- `new_note` (): Notify on new note
- `notification_email` (string): Email address for notifications
- `push_to_merge_request` (): Notify on push to MR
- `reassign_issue` (): Notify on issue reassign
- `reassign_merge_request` (): Notify on MR reassign
- `reopen_issue` (): Notify on issue reopen
- `reopen_merge_request` (): Notify on MR reopen
- `success_pipeline` (): Notify on pipeline success

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### package (8 tools)

#### gitlab_package_delete

Delete a package and all its files from the GitLab Package Registry. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_package_list, gitlab_package_file_delete

Returns: confirmation message.

**Parameters:**

- `package_id` (string) (required): Package ID to delete,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_package_download

Download a file from the GitLab Generic Package Registry and save it to a local path. Returns the output path, file size, and SHA256 checksum.

See also: gitlab_package_list, gitlab_package_publish

Returns: JSON with output path, file size, and SHA256 checksum.

**Parameters:**

- `file_name` (string) (required): File name to download,required
- `output_path` (string) (required): Absolute path where the file will be saved on the local filesystem,required
- `package_name` (string) (required): Package name,required
- `package_version` (string) (required): Package version,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_package_file_delete

Delete a single file from a package in the GitLab Package Registry. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_package_file_list, gitlab_package_delete

Returns: confirmation message.

**Parameters:**

- `package_file_id` (string) (required): Package file ID to delete,required
- `package_id` (string) (required): Package ID,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_package_file_list

List files within a specific package. Returns file ID, name, size, and SHA256 for each file with pagination.

See also: gitlab_package_list, gitlab_package_download

Returns: JSON array of package files with pagination.

**Parameters:**

- `package_id` (string) (required): Package ID,required
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_package_list

List packages in a GitLab project. Can filter by name, version, type, and supports pagination and sorting.

See also: gitlab_package_file_list, gitlab_package_publish

Returns: JSON array of packages with pagination.

**Parameters:**

- `include_versionless` (boolean): Include versionless packages
- `order_by` (string): Order by: name, created_at, version, type
- `package_name` (string): Filter by package name
- `package_type` (string): Filter by type (generic, npm, maven, etc.)
- `package_version` (string): Filter by package version
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `sort` (string): Sort direction: asc or desc
- `status` (string): Filter by status: default, hidden, processing, error

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_package_publish

Upload a single file to the GitLab Generic Package Registry. Provide either file_path (absolute local path) or content_base64 (base64-encoded content), not both. Returns the package file ID, size, SHA256, and the real download URL in the 'url' field. Use that 'url' value with gitlab_release_link_cre...

**Parameters:**

- `content_base64` (string): Base64-encoded file content. Only one should be provided.
- `file_name` (string) (required): Name of the file within the package,required
- `file_path` (string): Absolute path to a local file. Alternative to content_base64. Only one of file_path or content_base64 should be provided...
- `package_name` (string) (required): Package name (alphanumeric, dots, dashes, underscores),required
- `package_version` (string) (required): Package version (e.g. 1.0.0),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `status` (string): Package status: default or hidden

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_package_publish_and_link

Upload a file to the Generic Package Registry and create a release asset link pointing to it in one step (recommended over separate publish + link calls). Provide either file_path or content_base64 for the file content. The release identified by tag_name must already exist. Automatically uses the re...

**Parameters:**

- `content_base64` (string): Base64-encoded file content. Alternative to file_path.
- `file_name` (string) (required): Name of the file within the package,required
- `file_path` (string): Absolute path to a local file. Alternative to content_base64.
- `link_name` (string): Display name of the release link. Defaults to file_name if omitted. MUST be the exact filename â€” never add descriptive...
- `link_type` (string): Type of the release link: package, runbook, image, or other. Defaults to package.
- `package_name` (string) (required): Package name (alphanumeric, dots, dashes, underscores),required
- `package_version` (string) (required): Package version (e.g. 1.0.0),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `status` (string): Package status: default or hidden
- `tag_name` (string) (required): Tag name of the release to link the package file to,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_package_publish_directory

Batch-upload all matching files from a local directory to the Generic Package Registry. Walks the directory (non-recursive), filters by an optional glob pattern (e.g. *.tar.gz, *.exe), and publishes each file. Ideal for uploading release binaries or build artifacts in bulk. Returns the list of publi...

**Parameters:**

- `directory_path` (string) (required): Absolute path to a local directory whose files will be published,required
- `include_pattern` (string): Glob pattern to filter files within the directory (e.g. *.tar.gz). If omitted, all regular files are included.
- `package_name` (string) (required): Package name (alphanumeric, dots, dashes, underscores),required
- `package_version` (string) (required): Package version (e.g. 1.0.0),required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `status` (string): Package status: default or hidden

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

### pages (9 tools)

#### gitlab_pages_domain_create

Create a new Pages domain for a project. Optionally configure SSL certificate.

Returns: JSON with the created Pages domain details.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_get, gitlab_pages_domain_delete

**Parameters:**

- `auto_ssl_enabled` (): Enable automatic SSL certificate provisioning
- `certificate` (string): PEM-encoded SSL certificate
- `domain` (string) (required): Custom domain name (e.g. example.com),required
- `key` (string): PEM-encoded private key for the certificate
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pages_domain_delete

Delete a Pages domain from a project.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_create

**Parameters:**

- `domain` (string) (required): The Pages domain name to delete,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pages_domain_get

Get a single Pages domain for a project, including certificate details.

Returns: JSON with Pages domain details including SSL certificate information.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_update, gitlab_pages_domain_delete

**Parameters:**

- `domain` (string) (required): The Pages domain name,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pages_domain_list

List Pages domains for a specific project. Supports pagination.

Returns: JSON array of Pages domains with pagination.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_get, gitlab_pages_domain_create

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pages_domain_list_all

List all Pages domains across all projects accessible to the authenticated user.

Returns: JSON array of Pages domains.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_list

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pages_domain_update

Update an existing Pages domain for a project. Can update SSL settings.

Returns: JSON with the updated Pages domain details.

See also: gitlab_pages_domain_get

**Parameters:**

- `auto_ssl_enabled` (): Enable automatic SSL certificate provisioning
- `certificate` (string): PEM-encoded SSL certificate
- `domain` (string) (required): The Pages domain name to update,required
- `key` (string): PEM-encoded private key for the certificate
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pages_get

Get Pages settings for a project. Returns URL, unique domain status, HTTPS enforcement, deployments, and primary domain.

Returns: JSON with Pages configuration and deployment details.

See also: gitlab_pages_update, gitlab_pages_unpublish

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pages_unpublish

Unpublish Pages for a project. Removes all published Pages content.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_pages_get

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pages_update

Update Pages settings for a project. Can configure unique domain, HTTPS enforcement, and primary domain.

Returns: JSON with the updated Pages settings.

See also: gitlab_pages_get

**Parameters:**

- `pages_https_only` (): Enforce HTTPS for Pages
- `pages_primary_domain` (string): Primary domain for Pages
- `pages_unique_domain_enabled` (): Enable unique domain for Pages
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### personal (6 tools)

#### gitlab_personal_access_token_get

Get a personal access token by ID. Use token_id=0 to retrieve the current token used for authentication.

See also: gitlab_personal_access_token_list, gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate

Returns: JSON with access token details.

**Parameters:**

- `token_id` (integer) (required): Access token ID (required, use 0 for current token)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_personal_access_token_list

List personal access tokens. Filter by state, search by name, or filter by user ID (admin only).

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_list, gitlab_group_access_token_list

Returns: JSON array of access tokens with pagination.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `search` (string): Search by token name
- `state` (string): Token state filter: active, inactive
- `user_id` (integer): Filter by user ID (admin only)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_personal_access_token_revoke

Revoke a personal access token by ID. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_personal_access_token_list, gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate

Returns: confirmation message.

**Parameters:**

- `token_id` (integer) (required): Access token ID to revoke,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_personal_access_token_revoke_self

Revoke the personal access token used for the current request. This action cannot be undone.

See also: gitlab_personal_access_token_revoke, gitlab_personal_access_token_list

Returns: confirmation message.

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate

Rotate a personal access token, generating a new token value. Optionally set a new expiry date.

See also: gitlab_personal_access_token_list, gitlab_personal_access_token_revoke

Returns: JSON with the rotated access token including the new token value.

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): New expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- `token_id` (integer) (required): Access token ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate_self

Rotate the personal access token used for the current request. Returns the new token value.

See also: gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate, gitlab_project_access_token_rotate_self

Returns: JSON with the rotated access token including the new token value.

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): New expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### pipeline (29 tools)

#### gitlab_pipeline_cancel

Cancel a running pipeline in a GitLab project. Returns the updated pipeline details with canceled status.

Returns: pipeline id, iid, status, ref, sha, web_url, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_retry, gitlab_pipeline_get.

**Parameters:**

- `pipeline_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline ID to act on,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_create

Create a new pipeline for a branch or tag. Optionally pass variables (key/value pairs with type env_var or file). Returns: id, iid, status, source, ref, sha, web_url, duration, coverage, timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get, gitlab_job_list.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string) (required): Branch or tag name to run the pipeline for
- `variables` (): Pipeline variables to set

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_delete

Permanently delete a pipeline and all its jobs. This action cannot be undone. Requires at least Maintainer access level.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_pipeline_list.

**Parameters:**

- `pipeline_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline ID to delete,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_get

Retrieve detailed information about a specific pipeline in a GitLab project. Returns pipeline ID, status, source, ref, SHA, duration, coverage, user, timestamps, and YAML errors. See also: gitlab_job_list, gitlab_pipeline_test_report.

Returns: id, iid, status, source, ref, sha, duration, coverage, ...

**Parameters:**

- `pipeline_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline ID to retrieve,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_latest

Get the latest pipeline for a project, optionally filtered by branch/tag ref. Returns full pipeline details.

Returns: id, iid, status, source, ref, sha, duration, web_url, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get, gitlab_job_list.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string): Branch or tag name to filter by (defaults to the default branch)

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_list

List pipelines for a GitLab project. Supports filtering by status (success, failed, running, pending, canceled), scope (running, pending, finished, branches, tags), source (push, web, schedule, merge_request_event), ref (branch/tag), SHA, and username. Returns pipeline ID, status, source, ref, web U...

**Parameters:**

- `created_after` (string): Return pipelines created after date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- `created_before` (string): Return pipelines created before date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z)
- `name` (string): Filter by pipeline name
- `order_by` (string): Order by field (id, status, ref, updated_at, user_id)
- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string): Filter by branch or tag name
- `scope` (string): Filter by scope (running, pending, finished, branches, tags)
- `sha` (string): Filter by commit SHA
- `sort` (string): Sort direction (asc, desc)
- `source` (string): Filter by source (push, web, trigger, schedule, api, external, pipeline, chat, merge_request_event)
- `status` (string): Filter by status (created, waiting_for_resource, preparing, pending, running, success, failed, canceled, skipped, manual...
- `updated_after` (string): Return pipelines updated after date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- `updated_before` (string): Return pipelines updated before date (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z)
- `username` (string): Filter by username that triggered the pipeline
- `yaml_errors` (boolean): Return only pipelines with YAML errors

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_retry

Retry only the failed jobs in a pipeline (successful jobs are not re-run). Returns the updated pipeline details. To retry a specific job, use gitlab_job_retry instead.

Returns: pipeline id, iid, status, ref, sha, web_url, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_get, gitlab_job_list.

**Parameters:**

- `pipeline_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline ID to act on,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_create

Create a new pipeline schedule in a GitLab project. Requires description, ref (branch/tag), and cron expression. Optionally set timezone and active state. Returns: id, description, ref, cron, cron_timezone, next_run_at, active, owner_name. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get.

**Parameters:**

- `active` (): Whether the schedule is active (default: true)
- `cron` (string) (required): Cron expression (e.g. 0 1 * * *),required
- `cron_timezone` (string): Cron timezone (e.g. UTC or America/New_York)
- `description` (string) (required): Schedule description,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string) (required): Branch or tag to run the pipeline on,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_create_variable

Create a new variable for a pipeline schedule. Variables are passed to pipelines triggered by the schedule. Supports env_var (default) and file types. Returns: key, value, variable_type. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get.

**Parameters:**

- `key` (string) (required): Variable key,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `schedule_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline schedule ID,required
- `value` (string) (required): Variable value,required
- `variable_type` (string): Variable type: env_var (default) or file

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_delete

Permanently delete a pipeline schedule from a GitLab project. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_list.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `schedule_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline schedule ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_delete_variable

Delete a pipeline schedule variable by key. This action cannot be undone.

Returns: confirmation message. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get.

**Parameters:**

- `key` (string) (required): Variable key to delete,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `schedule_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline schedule ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_edit_variable

Edit an existing pipeline schedule variable by key. Updates the value and optionally the variable type. Returns: key, value, variable_type. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get.

**Parameters:**

- `key` (string) (required): Variable key to edit,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `schedule_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline schedule ID,required
- `value` (string) (required): New variable value,required
- `variable_type` (string): Variable type: env_var or file

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get

Get details of a specific pipeline schedule in a GitLab project by its ID. Returns description, ref, cron expression, timezone, active state, owner, and timestamps.

Returns: id, description, ref, cron, cron_timezone, active, next_run_at, owner, and timestamps. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_lis...

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `schedule_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline schedule ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_list

List pipeline schedules for a GitLab project. Supports filtering by scope (active, inactive). Returns paginated results with schedule details.

Returns: paginated list of schedules with id, description, ref, cron, active state, and next_run_at. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get, gitlab_pipeline...

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `scope` (string): Filter by scope: active or inactive

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_list_triggered_pipelines

List all pipelines that were triggered by a specific pipeline schedule. Returns paginated results with pipeline ID, ref, status, and source.

Returns: paginated list of pipelines with id, ref, status, and source. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get, gitlab_pipeline_get.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number for pagination
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `schedule_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline schedule ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_run

Trigger an immediate run of a pipeline schedule. Executes the schedule now regardless of its cron timing. Returns the updated schedule details.

Returns: id, description, ref, cron, cron_timezone, active, next_run_at, and owner. See also: gitlab_pipeline_list.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `schedule_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline schedule ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_take_ownership

Take ownership of a pipeline schedule, making the current user the owner. Returns the updated schedule details.

Returns: id, description, ref, cron, cron_timezone, active, next_run_at, and owner. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_list.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `schedule_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline schedule ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_schedule_update

Update an existing pipeline schedule in a GitLab project. All fields are optional: description, ref, cron, timezone, active state. Returns: id, description, ref, cron, cron_timezone, next_run_at, active, owner_name. See also: gitlab_pipeline_schedule_get.

**Parameters:**

- `active` (): Enable or disable the schedule
- `cron` (string): Updated cron expression
- `cron_timezone` (string): Updated cron timezone
- `description` (string): Updated description
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `ref` (string): Updated branch or tag
- `schedule_id` (integer) (required): Pipeline schedule ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_pipeline_security_summary

Get security report summary for a pipeline (requires Ultimate/Premium). Returns: scanner-level breakdown (SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, container scanning, secret detection, coverage fuzzing, API fuzzing, cluster image scanning) with vulnerability counts.

**Parameters:**

- `pipeline_iid` (string) (required): Pipeline IID (internal ID within the project),required
- `project_path` (string) (required): Full path of the project (e.g. my-group/my-project),required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

### project (78 tools)

#### gitlab_project_access_token_create

Create a new project access token with specified name, scopes, access level, and optional expiry date.

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_list, gitlab_group_access_token_create

Returns: JSON with the created access token including the token value.

**Parameters:**

- `access_level` (integer): Access level: 10 (guest), 20 (reporter), 30 (developer), 40 (maintainer)
- `description` (string): Token description
- `expires_at` (string): Expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- `name` (string) (required): Token name,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `scopes` () (required): Token scopes: api, read_api, read_repository, write_repository, etc.,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_access_token_get

Get a specific project access token by its ID.

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_list, gitlab_project_access_token_rotate

Returns: JSON with access token details.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `token_id` (integer) (required): Access token ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_access_token_list

List all access tokens for a GitLab project. Filter by state (active, inactive).

See also: gitlab_group_access_token_list, gitlab_personal_access_token_list

Returns: JSON array of access tokens with pagination.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `state` (string): Token state filter: active, inactive

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

#### gitlab_project_access_token_rotate

Rotate a project access token, generating a new token value. Optionally set a new expiry date.

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_list, gitlab_project_access_token_revoke

Returns: JSON with the rotated access token including the new token value.

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): New expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `token_id` (integer) (required): Access token ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_access_token_rotate_self

Rotate the project access token used for the current request. Returns the new token value.

See also: gitlab_project_access_token_rotate, gitlab_personal_access_token_rotate_self

Returns: JSON with the rotated access token including the new token value.

**Parameters:**

- `expires_at` (string): New expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_add_push_rule

Add push rule configuration to a project. Enforce commit message format, branch naming, file size limits, secret detection, and signed commits.

Returns: JSON with the created push rule configuration.

See also: gitlab_project_get_push_rules.

**Parameters:**

- `author_email_regex` (string): Regex to validate author email addresses
- `branch_name_regex` (string): Regex to validate branch names
- `commit_committer_check` (): Reject commits where committer is not a project member
- `commit_committer_name_check` (): Reject commits where committer name does not match user name
- `commit_message_negative_regex` (string): Regex that commit messages must NOT match
- `commit_message_regex` (string): Regex that commit messages must match
- `deny_delete_tag` (): Deny tag deletion
- `file_name_regex` (string): Regex for disallowed file names
- `max_file_size` (): Maximum file size (MB). 0 means unlimited
- `member_check` (): Only allow commits from project members
- `prevent_secrets` (): Reject files that are likely to contain secrets
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `reject_non_dco_commits` (): Reject commits without DCO certification
- `reject_unsigned_commits` (): Reject commits that are not GPG signed

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_approval_config_change

Update the project-level merge request approval configuration. Only specified fields are modified.

Returns: JSON with updated approval settings.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_config_get.

**Parameters:**

- `approvals_before_merge` (): Number of approvals required before merge
- `disable_overriding_approvers_per_merge_request` (): Prevent overriding approvers per MR
- `merge_requests_author_approval` (): Allow MR author to approve their own MR
- `merge_requests_disable_committers_approval` (): Prevent MR committers from approving
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `require_reauthentication_to_approve` (): Require reauthentication to approve
- `reset_approvals_on_push` (): Reset approvals when new commits are pushed
- `selective_code_owner_removals` (): Only remove code owner approvals when relevant files change

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_approval_config_get

Get the project-level merge request approval configuration including required approvals, author self-approval, committer approval, and password requirements.

Returns: JSON with approval settings.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_config_change, gitlab_project_approval_rule_list.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_approval_rule_create

Create a new project-level approval rule with the specified number of required approvals and approver groups/users.

Returns: JSON with the created approval rule details.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_rule_list, gitlab_project_approval_rule_update.

**Parameters:**

- `applies_to_all_protected_branches` (): Apply this rule to all protected branches
- `approvals_required` (integer) (required): Number of approvals required,required
- `group_ids` (): Group IDs to assign as approvers
- `name` (string) (required): Rule name,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `protected_branch_ids` (): Protected branch IDs to scope the rule to
- `rule_type` (string): Rule type (regular, code_owner)
- `user_ids` (): User IDs to assign as approvers
- `usernames` (): Usernames to assign as approvers

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_approval_rule_delete

Delete a project-level approval rule.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_rule_list, gitlab_project_approval_rule_create.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `rule_id` (integer) (required): Approval rule ID to delete,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_approval_rule_get

Get details of a specific project-level approval rule.

Returns: JSON with approval rule details including name, approvals_required, eligible approvers, and protected branches.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_rule_list, gitlab_project_approval_rule_update.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `rule_id` (integer) (required): Approval rule ID,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_approval_rule_list

List project-level approval rules. Each rule specifies the number of required approvals and eligible approvers.

Returns: JSON array of approval rules with pagination.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_rule_get, gitlab_project_approval_rule_create.

**Parameters:**

- `page` (integer): Page number (default 1)
- `per_page` (integer): Items per page (default 20, max 100)
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=true, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_approval_rule_update

Update an existing project-level approval rule. Only specified fields are modified.

Returns: JSON with the updated approval rule details.

See also: gitlab_project_approval_rule_get, gitlab_project_approval_rule_delete.

**Parameters:**

- `applies_to_all_protected_branches` (): Apply this rule to all protected branches
- `approvals_required` (): Updated number of approvals required
- `group_ids` (): Updated group IDs to assign as approvers
- `name` (string): Updated rule name
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
- `protected_branch_ids` (): Updated protected branch IDs
- `rule_id` (integer) (required): Approval rule ID,required
- `user_ids` (): Updated user IDs to assign as approvers
- `usernames` (): Updated usernames to assign as approvers

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_archive

Archive a GitLab project, making it read-only. Archived projects are hidden from default project list.

Returns: JSON with updated project details. See also: gitlab_project_unarchive, gitlab_project_get.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_create

Create a new GitLab project (repository). Supports setting namespace, visibility (private/internal/public), description, default branch, optional README initialization, merge method, squash option, topics, and feature flags (issues_enabled, merge_requests_enabled, wiki_enabled, jobs_enabled, lfs_ena...

**Parameters:**

- `allow_merge_on_skipped_pipeline` (): Allow merge when pipeline is skipped
- `autoclose_referenced_issues` (): Auto-close referenced issues on merge
- `build_timeout` (integer): Build timeout in seconds
- `ci_config_path` (string): Custom CI/CD configuration file path
- `ci_forward_deployment_enabled` (): Enable CI/CD forward deployment
- `container_registry_access_level` (string): Container registry access level (disabled, private, enabled)
- `default_branch` (string): Default branch name
- `description` (string): Project description
- `import_url` (string): URL to import repository from
- `initialize_with_readme` (boolean): Initialize with a README
- `issues_enabled` (): Enable issues feature
- `jobs_enabled` (): Enable CI/CD jobs
- `lfs_enabled` (): Enable Git LFS
- `merge_method` (string): Merge method (merge, rebase_merge, ff)
- `merge_requests_enabled` (): Enable merge requests feature
- `name` (string) (required): Project name,required
- `namespace_id` (integer): Namespace ID (defaults to personal namespace)
- `only_allow_merge_if_all_discussions_are_resolved` (boolean): Only allow merge when all discussions are resolved
- `only_allow_merge_if_pipeline_succeeds` (boolean): Only allow merge when pipeline succeeds
- `package_registry_access_level` (string): Package registry access level (disabled, private, enabled)
- `packages_enabled` (): Enable packages feature (deprecated: use package_registry_access_level)
- `pages_access_level` (string): Pages access level (disabled, private, enabled, public)
- `path` (string): Project path slug (defaults from name)
- `public_builds` (): Enable public access to pipelines
- `remove_source_branch_after_merge` (): Remove source branch after merge by default
- `request_access_enabled` (): Allow users to request access
- `shared_runners_enabled` (): Enable shared runners
- `snippets_access_level` (string): Snippets access level (disabled, private, enabled)
- `squash_option` (string): Squash option (never, always, default_on, default_off)
- `suggestion_commit_message` (string): Default commit message for suggestions
- `topics` (): Topic tags for the project
- `visibility` (string): Visibility level (private, internal, public)
- `wiki_enabled` (): Enable wiki feature

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_create_for_user

Create a new project owned by the specified user (admin operation). The project is created in the target user's personal namespace by default.

Returns: JSON with the created project details.

See also: gitlab_project_create, gitlab_project_get.

**Parameters:**

- `default_branch` (string): Default branch name
- `description` (string): Project description
- `initialize_with_readme` (boolean): Initialize with a README
- `issues_enabled` (): Enable issues feature
- `jobs_enabled` (): Enable CI/CD jobs
- `merge_requests_enabled` (): Enable merge requests feature
- `name` (string) (required): Project name,required
- `namespace_id` (integer): Namespace ID (defaults to user personal namespace)
- `path` (string): Project path slug (defaults from name)
- `topics` (): Topic tags for the project
- `user_id` (integer) (required): Target user ID who will own the project,required
- `visibility` (string): Visibility level (private, internal, public)
- `wiki_enabled` (): Enable wiki feature

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_create_fork_relation

Create a fork relation between two existing projects. This makes the project appear as forked from the specified source project.

Returns: JSON with fork relation details including forked_to_project_id and forked_from_project_id.

See also: gitlab_project_fork, gitlab_project_delete_fork_relation.

**Parameters:**

- `forked_from_id` (integer) (required): ID of the project to set as the fork source,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path of the forked project,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=false, idempotent=false, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_delete

Delete a GitLab project. On instances with delayed deletion enabled, the project is marked for deletion (scheduled) rather than removed immediately â€” the response includes the scheduled deletion date. Set permanently_remove=true with full_path to bypass delayed deletion and permanently remove the ...

**Parameters:**

- `full_path` (string): Full path of the project to confirm permanent removal (required when permanently_remove is true)
- `permanently_remove` (boolean): If true, immediately and permanently delete the project bypassing delayed deletion. Requires admin permissions on some G...
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_delete_fork_relation

Remove the fork relation from a project. The project will no longer be marked as a fork.

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_create_fork_relation.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

#### gitlab_project_delete_push_rule

Returns: confirmation message.

See also: gitlab_project_get_push_rules.

**Parameters:**

- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required

Annotations: readOnly=false, destructive=true, idempotent=true, openWorld=true

**Parameters:**

- `group_id` (integer) (required): Group ID to remove from project sharing,required
- `project_id` (string) (required): Project ID or URL-encoded path,required
